title: Sam Claflin

infobox.value: Samuel George Claflin   (1986-06-27) 27 June 1986 (age 39)  [Ipswich](/wiki/Ipswich "Ipswich"), Suffolk, England
[London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art](/wiki/London_Academy_of_Music_and_Dramatic_Art "London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art")

([/ˈklæflɪn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born 27 June 1986) is an English actor. After graduating from the [London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art](/wiki/London_Academy_of_Music_and_Dramatic_Art "London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art") in 2009, he began his acting career on television and had his first film role as [Philip Swift](/wiki/Philip_Swift "Philip Swift") in *[Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides](/wiki/Pirates_of_the_Caribbean:_On_Stranger_Tides "Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides")* (2011).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actor (born 1986)

| Sam Claflin | |
| --- | --- |
| Claflin in 2014 | |
| Born | Samuel George Claflin   (1986-06-27) 27 June 1986 (age 39)  [Ipswich](/wiki/Ipswich "Ipswich"), Suffolk, England |
| Alma mater | [London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art](/wiki/London_Academy_of_Music_and_Dramatic_Art "London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art") |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 2010–present |
| Spouse | [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock")  ​  ​ (m. 2013; sep. 2019)​ |
| Children | 2 |

**Samuel George Claflin** ([/ˈklæflɪn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born 27 June 1986) is an English actor. After graduating from the [London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art](/wiki/London_Academy_of_Music_and_Dramatic_Art "London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art") in 2009, he began his acting career on television and had his first film role as [Philip Swift](/wiki/Philip_Swift "Philip Swift") in *[Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides](/wiki/Pirates_of_the_Caribbean:_On_Stranger_Tides "Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides")* (2011).

Claflin gained wider recognition for playing [Finnick Odair](/wiki/Finnick_Odair "Finnick Odair") in [*The Hunger Games* film series](/wiki/The_Hunger_Games_(film_series) "The Hunger Games (film series)") (2013–2015) and for his starring roles in the romantic films *[Love, Rosie](/wiki/Love,_Rosie_(film) "Love, Rosie (film)")* (2014) and *[Me Before You](/wiki/Me_Before_You_(film) "Me Before You (film)")* (2016). He has since starred in the films *[Adrift](/wiki/Adrift_(2018_film) "Adrift (2018 film)")* (2018) and *[The Nightingale](/wiki/The_Nightingale_(2018_film) "The Nightingale (2018 film)")* (2018), and portrayed [Oswald Mosley](/wiki/Oswald_Mosley "Oswald Mosley") in the television series *[Peaky Blinders](/wiki/Peaky_Blinders_(TV_series) "Peaky Blinders (TV series)")* (2019–2022), [Mycroft Holmes](/wiki/Mycroft_Holmes "Mycroft Holmes") in the mystery film *[Enola Holmes](/wiki/Enola_Holmes_(film) "Enola Holmes (film)")* (2020), and singer Billy Dunne in the musical drama series *[Daisy Jones & the Six](/wiki/Daisy_Jones_%26_the_Six "Daisy Jones & the Six")* (2023). The last of these earned him a nomination for a [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award "Golden Globe Award").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Claflin&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Samuel George Claflin was born on 27 June 1986 in [Ipswich](/wiki/Ipswich "Ipswich"), Suffolk,[[1]](#cite_note-Biography-1) to Mark, a finance officer, and Sue Claflin (née Clarke), a teaching assistant. He has two older brothers, Benjamin and Daniel, and a younger brother, Joseph (born 1989), who is also an actor.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He grew up in [Norwich](/wiki/Norwich "Norwich"), Norfolk.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

As a child, he had an interest in [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"). He played throughout his childhood until he broke his ankle, an injury that he felt would prevent him from playing professionally. After some persuasion from his parents and a teacher upon whom he had made an impression during his high school play at [Costessey High School](/wiki/Ormiston_Victory_Academy "Ormiston Victory Academy"), he pursued acting.[[1]](#cite_note-Biography-1)[[4]](#cite_note-formercontessey-4)

A member of the [Norwich City](/wiki/Norwich_City_F.C. "Norwich City F.C.") School of Excellence,[[5]](#cite_note-5) in 2003, he took up Performing Arts at [Norwich City College](/wiki/Norwich_City_College "Norwich City College").[[4]](#cite_note-formercontessey-4) Claflin subsequently graduated from the [London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art](/wiki/London_Academy_of_Music_and_Dramatic_Art "London Academy of Music and Dramatic Art") in 2009.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Claflin&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Beginnings and breakthrough (2010–2015)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Claflin&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Beginnings and breakthrough (2010–2015)")]

Claflin made his acting debut in 2010, when he appeared in two television miniseries—in *[The Pillars of the Earth](/wiki/The_Pillars_of_the_Earth_(miniseries) "The Pillars of the Earth (miniseries)")* as Richard and in *[Any Human Heart](/wiki/Any_Human_Heart_(TV_series) "Any Human Heart (TV series)")* as young Logan Mountstuart. In March 2011, he was cast as Thomas in *[Seventh Son](/wiki/Seventh_Son_(film) "Seventh Son (film)")*, a film adaptation of *[The Spook's Apprentice](/wiki/The_Spook%27s_Apprentice "The Spook's Apprentice")*,[[7]](#cite_note-variety033111-7) but was dropped for unknown reasons and was replaced by [Ben Barnes](/wiki/Ben_Barnes_(actor) "Ben Barnes (actor)").[[8]](#cite_note-movieweb033111-8) His next appearance came in April 2011, when he portrayed footballer [Duncan Edwards](/wiki/Duncan_Edwards "Duncan Edwards") in the [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") TV drama *[United](/wiki/United_(2011_film) "United (2011 film)")*, which was centred on the events of the 1958 [Munich air disaster](/wiki/Munich_air_disaster "Munich air disaster"), in which Edwards died as a result of his injuries.

In April 2010, he was cast as [Philip Swift](/wiki/Philip_Swift "Philip Swift"), a missionary who falls in love with the mermaid Syrena (portrayed by [Àstrid Bergès-Frisbey](/wiki/%C3%80strid_Berg%C3%A8s-Frisbey "Àstrid Bergès-Frisbey")), in 2011's *[Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides](/wiki/Pirates_of_the_Caribbean:_On_Stranger_Tides "Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides")*, the fourth instalment of the [*Pirates of the Caribbean* film series](/wiki/Pirates_of_the_Caribbean_(film_series) "Pirates of the Caribbean (film series)"). For the film, he received a nomination in the [17th Empire Awards](/wiki/17th_Empire_Awards "17th Empire Awards") for "Best Male Newcomer."[[9]](#cite_note-Empire-9) In 2012, he appeared as Jack in the six-episode miniseries *[White Heat](/wiki/White_Heat_(miniseries) "White Heat (miniseries)")*. Later that year, he played a supporting role in the 2012 film *[Snow White and the Huntsman](/wiki/Snow_White_and_the_Huntsman "Snow White and the Huntsman")*, as William, Snow White's childhood friend. He received a nomination in the [2012 Teen Choice Awards](/wiki/2012_Teen_Choice_Awards "2012 Teen Choice Awards") for the category "Best Movie Breakout". Also in 2012, he was cast in the lead role in [Hammer Films](/wiki/Hammer_Films "Hammer Films")' *[The Quiet Ones](/wiki/The_Quiet_Ones_(2014_film) "The Quiet Ones (2014 film)")*, which was filmed in mid-2013 and was released in April 2014.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On 22 August 2012, [Lionsgate](/wiki/Lionsgate_Films "Lionsgate Films") announced that Claflin had been cast as [Finnick Odair](/wiki/Finnick_Odair "Finnick Odair") in [*The Hunger Games* film series](/wiki/The_Hunger_Games_(film_series) "The Hunger Games (film series)"), starting with *[The Hunger Games: Catching Fire](/wiki/The_Hunger_Games:_Catching_Fire "The Hunger Games: Catching Fire")*.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-USweeklyCasted-12) The director of *Catching Fire*, [Francis Lawrence](/wiki/Francis_Lawrence "Francis Lawrence"), stated of Claflin's performance during filming: "Finnick's an interesting character. At first he feels like a bit of a flirt and there's a little bit of sexual tension, but really you kind of fall in love with the guy and you see that there's a real deep emotional side to him. It's one of the reasons I really hired him in the first place was that it's where his character goes in the next couple of stories and I just think he did a fantastic job."[[13]](#cite_note-MTV-13) The film was released in November 2013 to general acclaim and Claflin's portrayal of Finnick received positive reviews.[[14]](#cite_note-Rotten-14)[[15]](#cite_note-CNN-15) Claflin reprised the role in *[The Hunger Games: Mockingjay – Part 1](/wiki/The_Hunger_Games:_Mockingjay_%E2%80%93_Part_1 "The Hunger Games: Mockingjay – Part 1")*, released in November 2014, and *[Part 2](/wiki/The_Hunger_Games:_Mockingjay_%E2%80%93_Part_2 "The Hunger Games: Mockingjay – Part 2")*, released in November 2015.[[16]](#cite_note-YahooMockingjay-16)

In February 2013, it was announced that Claflin was cast as Alex in the film adaptation of [Cecelia Ahern](/wiki/Cecelia_Ahern "Cecelia Ahern")'s novel *[Where Rainbows End](/wiki/Where_Rainbows_End "Where Rainbows End")*, distributed as [Love, Rosie](/wiki/Love,_Rosie_(film) "Love, Rosie (film)") in some territories.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The following month, he starred in the TV film *[Mary and Martha](/wiki/Mary_and_Martha_(film) "Mary and Martha (film)")* as Ben, which was broadcast by [BBC One](/wiki/BBC_One "BBC One").[[18]](#cite_note-18) He received critical acclaim for his role as [Oxford University](/wiki/Oxford_University "Oxford University") student Alistair Ryle in the British dramatic thriller *[The Riot Club](/wiki/The_Riot_Club "The Riot Club")* with Charlotte O'Sullivan of the *[London Evening Standard](/wiki/London_Evening_Standard "London Evening Standard")* saying "Claflin captures his character's vitriol very well" and Laura Dibdin of *[Digital Spy](/wiki/Digital_Spy "Digital Spy")* saying "Claflin brings intrigue and vulnerability to offset the sense of entitlement thus making Alistair the film's most fascinating character". In 2014, Claflin also starred alongside [Lily Collins](/wiki/Lily_Collins "Lily Collins") and [Suki Waterhouse](/wiki/Suki_Waterhouse "Suki Waterhouse") in the romantic comedy drama film *[Love, Rosie](/wiki/Love,_Rosie_(film) "Love, Rosie (film)")*, which was released to mixed reviews from critics but became a success among audiences.

### From *Me Before You* to independent films and television (2016–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Claflin&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: From Me Before You to independent films and television (2016–present)")]

In 2016, Claflin reprised his role as William, Snow White's childhood friend, in several scenes of the sequel *[The Huntsman: Winter's War](/wiki/The_Huntsman:_Winter%27s_War "The Huntsman: Winter's War")*. The same year, he starred in *[Me Before You](/wiki/Me_Before_You_(film) "Me Before You (film)")*, the film adaptation of the [novel of the same name](/wiki/Me_Before_You_(novel) "Me Before You (novel)"), in which he played William Traynor.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Claflin gained further critical acclaim for his role of Captain Stanhope in the war drama *[Journey's End](/wiki/Journey%27s_End_(2017_film) "Journey's End (2017 film)")* (2017) and *[Adrift](/wiki/Adrift_(2018_film) "Adrift (2018 film)")* (2018).

In 2018, Claflin joined the fifth season of the BBC television drama *[Peaky Blinders](/wiki/Peaky_Blinders_(TV_series) "Peaky Blinders (TV series)")*, as the British fascist politician [Oswald Mosley](/wiki/Oswald_Mosley "Oswald Mosley").[[20]](#cite_note-20) In 2019, he starred in *[The Corrupted](/wiki/The_Corrupted "The Corrupted")* and the [reboot](/wiki/Charlie%27s_Angels_(2019_film) "Charlie's Angels (2019 film)") of *[Charlie's Angels](/wiki/Charlie%27s_Angels_(franchise) "Charlie's Angels (franchise)")*.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) In September 2020, he co-starred in the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") original film *[Enola Holmes](/wiki/Enola_Holmes_(film) "Enola Holmes (film)")* as Mycroft Holmes, elder brother of the title character.

In 2023, Claflin played Billy Dunne in *[Daisy Jones & the Six](/wiki/Daisy_Jones_%26_the_Six "Daisy Jones & the Six")*. Based on the book of the same name, it follows the story of a rock band in the 1970s, and premiered on [Amazon Prime Video](/wiki/Amazon_Prime_Video "Amazon Prime Video") on 3 March 2023.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Claflin performed vocals on *[Aurora](/wiki/Aurora_(Daisy_Jones_%26_The_Six_album) "Aurora (Daisy Jones & The Six album)")*, the fictional album recorded by the band, which was released on 1 March 2023. He starred in an Italian-British television [adaptation](/wiki/The_Count_of_Monte_Cristo_(2024_TV_series) "The Count of Monte Cristo (2024 TV series)") of [Alexandre Dumas](/wiki/Alexandre_Dumas "Alexandre Dumas")'s novel *[The Count of Monte-Cristo](/wiki/The_Count_of_Monte_Cristo_(2024_series) "The Count of Monte Cristo (2024 series)")* (1844–1846).[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sam_Claflin&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 2011, Claflin started dating actress [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock"),[[25]](#cite_note-25) whom he had met in an audition for *[My Week with Marilyn](/wiki/My_Week_with_Marilyn "My Week with Marilyn")*. The two married in July 2013 in a private ceremony.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) They have a son and a daughter.[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) On 20 August 2019, Claflin and Haddock announced their legal separation.[[31]](#cite_note-31)
